title: pterolobium micranthum
intro: Pterolobium micranthum, also known as the Karaya tree or Red Karaya, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Asia and India. This tree has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pterolobium micranthum include its potential to treat various conditions, such as diarrhea, dysentery, and skin infections, and it may also help in wound healing and reducing fever. Pterolobium micranthum is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is valued in traditional medicine for its multiple uses and benefits.
